title: B2C electronically supplied services are taxed where the EU consumer resides
conditionSummary: customer.type == consumer AND component.classification == ESS AND customer.country in EU

title: Human intervention is a classification signal, not an automatic ESS exclusion
conditionSummary: component.automationLevel != automated OR component.humanInvolvement == substantial -> flag likely non-ESS and require alternative-classification review; do not treat human involvement alone as conclusive

title: Non-EU supplier of B2C ESS owes destination VAT from the first covered sale; non-Union OSS is optional
conditionSummary: seller.country not in EU AND seller.euFixedEstablishment == false AND exists(component.classification == ESS AND customer.type == consumer AND customer.country in EU) -> destination VAT obligation; recommend either optional non-Union OSS or relevant local registrations; do not apply the intra-EU EUR 10,000 threshold

title: Customer-location evidence: normally two items of non-contradictory evidence
conditionSummary: R1 triggered -> request two non-contradictory items such as billing address, IP/geolocation, bank details, SIM or other commercial evidence; missing or conflicting evidence -> missing fact
notes: Article 24b contains a narrow one-item exception for certain EU-established suppliers under the EUR 100,000 threshold; it does not fit the Serbian-supplier fixture.

title: French standard VAT rate 20% (illustrative only)
conditionSummary: R1 triggered AND customer.country == FR -> show 20% as illustrative, never as a final liability calculation

title: B2B services under the general rule are supplied where the customer is established
conditionSummary: customer.type == business AND customer.country in EU AND service falls under the Article 44 general rule

title: Reverse charge: German business recipient accounts for VAT for an Article 44 service from Serbia
conditionSummary: R6 triggered AND seller not established in Germany -> customer accounts for VAT under Article 196 and UStG Section 13b(2)(1) together with Section 13b(5); seller does not charge German VAT

title: Invoice requirements include customer VAT ID and the words 'Reverse charge'
conditionSummary: R7 triggered -> invoice checklist includes customer VAT ID where required, the words 'Reverse charge', and no German VAT charged

title: Customer VAT ID must be validated through official VIES; an unvalidated ID downgrades the analysis
conditionSummary: customer.vatIdProvided == true AND viesValidation != valid -> Requires verification; viesValidation == valid -> supports, but does not alone conclusively establish, B2B treatment

title: Human-delivered B2C components require separate place-of-supply review
conditionSummary: customer.type == consumer AND component.classification in [training, consulting, support-human] -> apply different place-of-supply analysis from ESS and flag for professional review
notes: Article 45 is only the B2C general rule. Specific exceptions, including Article 59 for some non-EU customers, can override it; do not encode a final conclusion.

title: Serbian VAT place of supply follows the recipient for B2B services and for B2C electronically supplied services
conditionSummary: seller.country == RS AND ((customer.type == business AND Article 12 paragraph 4 general rule applies) OR (customer.type == consumer AND component.classification == ESS AND Article 12 paragraph 6 point 7 subpoint 12 applies)) -> place of supply is the recipient location; if S13 is unavailable, disable R11
notes: S13 was found in the official consolidated Serbian VAT Act through amendment 109/25. While humanReviewStatus is pending, output remains draft/research-only and visibly marked Pending human review.

title: Software-rights transfer in Serbia-Germany B2B raises a withholding-tax and treaty-classification question
conditionSummary: component.ipRightsTransferred == true -> raise a potential Article 13 royalty/WHT touchpoint; never classify software consideration or calculate withholding conclusively
notes: The 1987 Germany-Yugoslavia treaty continues to apply to Serbia according to the official country page; the software-payment classification remains for professional review.
